topic: A person discovers their partner has been taking secret phone calls
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment had become a heavy, suffocating thing. I watched Mark across the dinner table, his eyes darting toward the hallway every few minutes, a nervous tic I’d noticed only recently. He always claimed he was just taking work calls, but the timing was always odd—late at night, or when I was preoccupied with a project. Tonight, the sound of his phone vibrating under the table made my fork clatter against the plate. I waited, my jaw tightening, watching the screen illuminate with an unknown number. When he finally ended the call, he offered a breezy, almost too-quick smile. I didn't say anything. I just picked up my wine glass, the crystal feeling cold and sharp in my hand, and took a long, deliberate sip, trying to keep the tremor in my fingers from showing.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked deep inside the drawer of his desk, a burner phone that wasn't his. The realization hit me not as a sudden explosion, but as a slow, chilling drop in temperature. I picked up the device, the plastic cool against my palm, and felt a strange, brittle certainty settle in my stomach. I scrolled through the recent call logs, the timestamps lining up with evenings I’d been alone, or when he claimed to be working late at the office. I didn't confront him immediately. Instead, I sat in the living room, the ambient light feeling too bright, and meticulously cataloged the data, my breathing shallow and even. Every incoming notification felt like a tiny, deliberate accusation.

[story 3]

It started with the subtle shifts—the way he’d suddenly become engrossed in his phone, his voice dropping to a tone I didn't recognize, a low murmur that didn't belong to our shared space. I’d tried to brush it off, attributing it to stress or a demanding client, but the pattern persisted. One Tuesday, I overheard a snippet of conversation, a clipped exchange punctuated by laughter that sounded distant and unfamiliar. My hands clenched into fists at my sides beneath the table. I looked at him then, really looked, searching for the flicker of honesty I usually relied on. When he met my gaze, there was a swift evasion, a momentary blankness that told me everything I needed to know without a single word spoken between us.
